E El Paso County, Colorado
Dashboard Property lookup Sources
Strategic Performance Dashboard

A dynamic display of strategic plan objectives and performance.

In a quick snapshot, it visualizes and simplifies the complexity of County efforts in an easy-to-understand, transparent way. It shows progress, trends, and how we arrive at data-driven decision-making.

Infrastructure Service Quality Community Trust Health & Safety
Pillar 01 · Infrastructure

Build, maintain, and improve what we already own.

Sustainably fund, manage, and improve public-owned infrastructure. Six asset classes tracked; one operational layer proposed.

$59.1M 2026 Roads & Infrastructure budget
Bridge Sufficiency Rating On Track
91
Goal range 80–100 · 285 bridges inspected

El Paso County regularly inspects its 285 bridges to ensure safety and prevent costly damage. Bridges are rated on a sufficiency scale from 0 to 100, assessing structural integrity and functionality.

Regular inspections and maintenance extend the lifespan of these vital structures, reducing the risk of failure.

Drill down View detail
Pavement Condition Index On Track
71 / 100 PCI
Goal range 55–85 · 2,429 lane miles · $3B asset value

The Department of Public Works monitors and maintains the County's vast paved roadway network — representing a $3 billion asset. Conditions are measured every three years using advanced 3D laser scanning, GPS, and roughness sensors.

This data prioritizes maintenance, rehabilitation, and reconstruction. The goal is to increase PCI by one point each year — 72 would move the network into "good" territory.

Drill down Look up a property
Stormwater Control Measures On Track
79
Goal range 70–90 · 30 permanent control measures

The County operates 30 Permanent Control Measures designed to reduce pollutant discharge into local streams and rivers. Public Works inspects them annually for MS4 permit compliance.

Proper maintenance reduces flooding risk, protects waterways, and avoids costly non-compliance fines.

Drill down View detail
Fleet Readiness On Track
95 %
Goal range 80–100 · 21 organizations supported

Fleet Management provides transportation and construction equipment to 21 organizations across the County. "Readiness" measures the percentage of time vehicles are available versus unavailable for maintenance.

Maintained consistently above the 95% target through proactive maintenance and replacement programs.

Drill down View detail
Facilities Condition On Track
90 %
Goal range 87–95% · Life Remaining Scale

The Facilities Condition Index assesses building condition by comparing deferred maintenance costs to each building's replacement value. The County uses the Life Remaining Scale — 90% means "On Track."

Tracked via the MyFacilityInformer tool to prioritize maintenance based on available resources.

Drill down View detail
Park Assets Condition Measuring
In development
Goal range to be determined

Parks & Community Services is implementing a Parks Condition Index to systematically assess trails, sport fields and courts, playgrounds, and other assets.

A new Computerized Maintenance Management System will streamline maintenance tasks once the baseline assessment is complete.

Drill down View detail
Road Service Operations Not Published
Proposed addition
Operational KPIs not currently published

The published Infrastructure KPIs measure asset condition. They don't measure operational execution — how the $59.1M actually gets converted into truck rolls and repairs in the field.

Adding these four metrics would require no new staff, no new asphalt, no new vehicles. The data already exists in the County's internal work-order system.

Avg. response time
Not published
Avg. resolution time
Not published
Bundling rate
Not published
Repairs per dispatch
Not published
Proposal Read the proposal
Pillar 02 · Service Quality

Improve service quality with an engaged workforce.

Two KPIs: how the public experiences County services, and how employees experience their work.

2 of 2 KPIs tracked
Customer Experience Index Measuring
Baseline in development
Goal range to be determined

El Paso County values citizen and employee input to improve services. Current customer experience initiatives focus on Customer Satisfaction, Effort, and Advocacy as the three measurement dimensions.

This feedback is intended to align services with community expectations, though a public-facing score has not yet been published.

Drill down View detail
Employee Engagement Index On Track
78 %
Goal range 60–70% · Biannual survey

The County assesses how valued, connected, and supported employees feel through a biannual Engagement Index. Current data shows strong purpose alignment (90%) and role satisfaction (78%).

Improvement opportunities identified in career growth and development pathways.

Drill down View detail
Pillar 03 · Community Trust

Strengthen trust through communication and transparency.

A single composite index built from four factors: civility, transparency, capability, and reliability.

4 factors Index in development
Community Trust Index Measuring
Composite in development
Four factors based on Edelman Trust research

Community Trust serves as the cornerstone of a successful relationship between the County and its stakeholders. It is fostered through consistent actions that reflect both intent and competence.

Leading research identifies four critical factors essential to building and maintaining community trust. Each will be measured independently and combined into the composite index.

Civility
In development
Transparency
In development
Capability
In development
Reliability
In development
Drill down View detail
Pillar 04 · Health & Safety

Partner to improve resident health and safety.

Develop partnerships to support community efforts to improve the health and safety of residents.

0 of TBD KPIs published
Health & Safety Pillar Not Published
Framework under development
No metrics, baselines, or goal ranges published yet

The Health and Safety pillar has been declared as a strategic priority but no public KPIs have been published yet. The framework, baseline measurements, and goal ranges are still under development.

This is the only pillar without a single published metric.

Status View detail

Sources & disclosures

  • KPI values, goal ranges, and narrative content are recreated from the official El Paso County Strategic Plan Dashboard.
  • Budget figures from the El Paso County 2026 Adopted Budget.
  • This page uses a single KPI tile template — every card follows the same structure. To update a metric, edit the corresponding <article class="kpi-card"> block and replace the label, value, goal, description, and drill-down link.
  • Status pill classes: on-track (green), measuring (amber), missing (red). Card variants: featured (active live drill-down), proposed (not currently published).
  • Drill-down links go to detail pages with methodology, sub-metrics, and related actions. The Pavement Condition Index card additionally links to a live parcel accountability lookup that queries the State of Colorado parcel API in real time.
  • This page is a citizen-built design demonstration. Not affiliated with El Paso County or its Department of Public Works.
